item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're going to start dropping insults like that, you're going to have to be way more specific on what you are talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea has potential, but the writing feels sloppy. I know this is in the contest, so it makes sense, but it still feels extremely rushed. Outside of the contest, this kind of post probably wouldn’t last long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is they are entering a zone where, metaphorically, the entire concept of art has been destroyed (the fake side supposedly contained a bunch of an-art pieces), and as a result are falling into a nightmare-filled coma where everything is mundane and bland and homogeneous. Once they get a piece of art nearby they snap right out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I’ve seen AWCY? give a “feck you” to the Foundation a couple times before, though I’d definitely give credit that I haven’t seen them create a whole site, heheh. I think this works better now with no anartists being mentioned before the wall art. My one thing is I don’t quite see the connection between comas and art? I understand it had to fit the theme for JamCon, but it feels thematically wobbly to me. Either way, nice work, and this has given me an idea…

item.title: Author Page
item.content:encoded:
This has changed a lot since the original idea - it's been trimmed back a lot and despite some resistance on my part I think it's better for it, so thanks to the people who pushed me to do it. The article was inspired by a Penny-Arcade strip and a random chat with Tuomey in #site19

Thanks to ihp, vezaz, DarkStuff, Othello and CaptainKirby for reading the draft!

ETA: Thanks to Taffeta for coming up with the title!

Image Credit
– hide block

Image is sourced from here - https://www.mildenhall.af.mil/News/Photos/igphoto/2000458193/
I was a little unsure about the licencing on this one but according to staff in #site17, because it's military it's open domain (which is how I found it, through an open licence search on google.)

You can find more of my work here!
